items.text: Hi Marcus, Your replacement student ID is ready for pickup at the Campus Card Office in Wells Hall, Room 108. Please stop by before Friday, April 12, and bring $10 in cash to cover the reprinting fee. Photo IDs are required. Thanks, Registrar Team

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Marcus bring to pick up the ID?
questions.explanation: The email asks him to bring $10 in cash to cover the reprinting fee, which matches 'ten dollars in cash.'

questions.stem: What else does the email say is required at pickup?
questions.explanation: The email states that photo IDs are required to collect the card.

items.text: Hi everyone, The Photography Club meeting has been moved. Instead of Tuesday at 6 PM, we'll now meet on Wednesday, May 8, at 5:30 PM in Baker Hall, Room 220. Please bring your camera and any printed photos for review. See you there, Elena

questions.stem: Where will the rescheduled meeting be held?
questions.explanation: The email says the group will now meet in Baker Hall, Room 220.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the club members?

options.A: They must pay a membership fee.
options.C: They take and print their own photos.
options.D: They are meeting for the first time.

questions.explanation: Because members are asked to bring their own cameras and printed photos, it can be inferred that they take and print photographs themselves.

items.text: Dear Priya, We received your request for a new student ID. The $10 replacement fee has been added to your account and must be paid online before we print the card. Pickup opens Monday, March 3, at the Student Services desk. Best, Card Services

questions.stem: According to the email, how should the fee be paid?
questions.explanation: The email states the fee must be paid online before the card is printed.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the new card?

options.A: It will be mailed to her home.
options.B: It will not be printed until payment.
options.C: It will expire at the end of March.
options.D: It will include a new photo.

questions.explanation: Since the fee must be paid before the card is printed, it can be inferred the card will not be produced until payment is made.

items.text: Just found the best study spot on campus — the library basement, Level B2! Super quiet, tons of outlets, and the vending machine has coffee for only $1.50. Open until midnight on weekdays. Grab a corner desk before finals week hits!

questions.stem: According to the post, how much does the coffee cost?
questions.explanation: The post says the vending machine sells coffee for only $1.50.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the study spot?

options.A: It is a good study environment.
options.B: It is reserved for graduate students.
options.C: It is only open on weekends.
options.D: It is far from the main entrance.

questions.explanation: The writer praises it as quiet with many outlets, so it can be inferred that it is a good place to study.
